🏆 Youth Sports Operations Platform

Run the team.
Not the chaos.

RallyIQ brings GameChanger stats, Band communication, NCS Fastpitch tournaments, roster sync, player profiles, and fundraising operations into one clean workflow for youth sports organizations.

View Screenshot Guide → Use Demo Voiceover
🔊 Turn up your device volume to unmute the reel Best with sound on

What RallyIQ does

One operating system for the whole organization.

The demo is built around the idea that directors run operations, coaches focus on player development, and families stay informed.

🧢

Coach workflow

Create practice plans, assign drills, set time blocks, and make player expectations visible before practice starts.

📊

Player profiles

Use stats, clips, photos, and tournament highlights to build a clear player development story over time.

💬

Family communication

Push tournament updates, practice reminders, jersey instructions, and schedule context through the tools families already use.

🏟️

Tournament discovery

Search NCS Fastpitch events by location, date, division, director, format, and team count.

🔁

Roster sync

Keep player names, numbers, positions, and platform connections organized across GameChanger, Band, and team tools.

🤝

Fundraising support

Track raffles, grants, sponsors, deadlines, local business outreach, and scholarship opportunities for families who need help.

Screenshot tour

Screen-by-screen demo guide.

Use this section as the clickable walkthrough when presenting RallyIQ to a coach, director, sponsor, or parent group.

Start here

Integration Hub

Connect GameChanger, Band, and NCS Fastpitch in one operational dashboard.

  • Position RallyIQ as the command center for the organization.
  • Explain that integrations reduce duplicate work across platforms.
  • Show that the system is designed around operations, not just one coach’s phone.
Hub view

Live Feed + Quick Actions

See connected platforms, tournament updates, and quick actions for stats, Band posts, tournaments, and roster sync.

  • Point out the live feed for tournament and platform updates.
  • Use the quick actions to show what a director or team admin can do fast.
  • Tie the workflow back to communication, stats, events, and roster maintenance.
Find events

Tournament Discovery

Search NCS Fastpitch by name, city, or date and review divisions, teams, directors, and format details.

  • Search by city, event name, or date to find relevant NCS Fastpitch events.
  • Show divisions, team counts, directors, locations, and event tags.
  • Explain that this helps families and coaches see the tournament plan clearly.
Add to app

Filtered NCS Results

Filter to Taylor events and quickly add the right tournament to the app or push event context to Band.

  • Filter down to the right city or division.
  • Use “Add to App” as the bridge from event discovery into team operations.
  • Use the Band button to frame how updates can become parent-facing messages.
Roster operations

Roster Sync

Unify roster records across platforms so player information, numbers, positions, and app icons stay aligned.

  • Show a clean roster with player numbers, names, initials, positions, and platform icons.
  • Explain that each player can become a profile with stats, photos, and video clips.
  • Frame this as the foundation for development, recruiting, and parent visibility.
Admin setup

Settings + Filters

Configure API keys, NCS team filters, age division, director filters, and GameChanger import settings.

  • Show that the platform can be configured by team, age division, and director.
  • Explain the GameChanger import path and NCS filtering.
  • Position settings as the admin layer that keeps the demo data relevant.

Demo narration

Simple AI voiceover script.

Paste this directly into an AI voiceover tool, or read it during the walkthrough.

🔊 Turn up your device volume to unmute the reel

This tool helps a youth sports organization stay organized.

Coaches can build practice plans, assign drills, set time blocks, and show every player what to expect before practice starts.

Parents and players can see the schedule, what jersey to wear, where to be, and what the team is working on.

The platform also connects with tools like GameChanger, Band, and NCS Fastpitch.

After games, stats and video clips can be pulled into the team portal.

Each player can have their own profile with stats, pictures, and highlight videos.

Instead of parents digging through GameChanger, everything is organized in one place.

The tool also helps with fundraising.

Teams can track raffle deadlines, sponsorships, grants, donations, and local business support.

A nonprofit or booster organization can use it to show exactly what the money is for and how it helps the kids.

For example, if a team is traveling to a tournament, the organization can explain the purpose behind the trip.

It is not just about playing games. It is about helping players learn teamwork, discipline, coachability, confidence, and responsibility.

The platform can also help identify players who need financial support.

That way, talented kids do not miss out just because their family cannot afford it.

In simple terms, this platform helps the organization handle the operations.

Coaches can focus on coaching. Players can focus on development. And families can stay informed.

Fundraising story

Turn donations into a plan sponsors understand.

RallyIQ helps organizations explain the purpose behind each funding request: tournament travel, player scholarships, equipment, mental-health resources, development programs, and local business sponsorships.

“If a player has the talent and desire to compete, money should not be the reason she loses the opportunity.”

Track the ask

Document raffle deadlines, sponsor targets, grant windows, and donation goals.

Explain the impact

Show exactly how the money supports player development, travel, gear, and family relief.

Support the right families

Let coaches nominate players for scholarships or fee support when money becomes the barrier.

Live demo flow

Six-minute run of show.

Use this structure to keep the demo tight and easy to follow.

Open on the Hub

Explain the problem: teams are scattered across GameChanger, Band, tournament sites, texts, and spreadsheets.

Show platform status

Point to connected platforms and explain that RallyIQ becomes the single operational layer.

Find a tournament

Search NCS Fastpitch, filter by city or division, and show how events can be added to the team workflow.

Sync the roster

Show how players are organized across platforms and how profiles can hold stats, clips, and player information.

Push communication

Explain how practice plans, tournament updates, uniform notes, and reminders can be pushed to families.

Close with fundraising

Connect the software story to grants, sponsorships, scholarships, and financial transparency for families.
